description: Great and powerful form, integrates with zapier and i can track visitors with google analytics. Smooth experience
author: Babatunde O.
pros: One of the free, no fluff online form builder I have used in a long while.
cons: Competitors were reporting my forms and it went offline, I had to explain to them to check that the form was not Spam, would have been nice if they checked first.

review_title: Simplified and unified form solution with exemplary customer service
description: Cognito's customer service is truly peerless. I cannot remember the last time I worked with such positive, dedicated solutions staff. Not only are they always available to answer any question, from billing to "how do I do this with the tools available", but they seem genuinely happy to help solve the puzzle that is your latest requirement, providing not only an example in your instance, but an explanation of how it was done. Or, if they cannot for whatever reason, they are happy to log an issue for a future feature for their team to develop.
author: Brandon S.
pros: Cognito unifies and simplifies our processes, providing tools that are accessible to an average user, but powerful enough to satisfy any of our needs. As a University, we have hundreds of forms, capturing everything from food allergies to personal grievances, to exam deferrals and Cognito has had a solution for all of them.
cons: Some common workflows still require complex several-step processes that should be simplified, such as approvals or guest roles. While there are workarounds, these are not as easy for average users to set up. Consolidating these functionalities into discrete, simplified pieces would make a huge improvement. Additionally, Cognito does not support some of the larger enterprise requirements, such as role based access control syncing with our identity management software; this means we have to manually maintain hundreds of users and try to keep this synced - a pain point that regrettably grows the more we use the platform.

review_title: 🔴 I strongly advise against using this company.
description: It was horrible. I had an issue with their login, so I could fix an issue, but no one could help me. I was losing money by the minute.
author: Alex L.
pros: It worked fine till it did not. I used them for years
cons: 🔴 I strongly advise against using this company. If your business relies on functional forms, it's crucial to have real-time customer service to address any issues that may arise. It's unacceptable for a company to operate without this support. In today's competitive business environment, companies with good customer service thrive, and I hope businesses like this one realize the importance of providing real-time support before they become obsolete like Blockbuster.

review_title: Cognito is a solid form option that I believe in
description: My overall experience has been very positive. So much so that I'm an affiliate and wholeheartedly recommend them to my clients. It's a powerful piece of software and almost anything you want to do can be done. The ease of use is a huge bonus for my clients who are not tech savvy.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I absolutely adore Cognito Forms. I use it myself and recommend it to all my clients. It's very easy to use - there's a drag and drop interface so you can build the form very quickly. You can add email notifications that go to the user as well as anyone else who needs to be notified. You can also redirect to a different page upon submission which is nice. If you want to do some advanced calculations and have conditional elements, that's also possible and quite powerful. This part does have a bit of a learning curve, but the majority of users will probably not need it. However, if you do get stuck, just reach out to their support, and they walk you through exactly what you need to do in order to create the form you want. I've got nothing but good things to say about their support team. Each form has a section where you can view entries and export whatever view you'd like. You can create a PDF template to send to your users if you're building things like purchase orders from the form input. You can accept payment as well which is really handy for one-off purchases. Subscriptions are not supported as far as I have seen, but it is easy to sell items or collect donations.
cons: There's not a whole lot to not like, really. One thing I'd love for them to do is to have some kind of global fields. I often build forms that need the same fields and while I can copy whole forms and edit those, sometimes you just need to add one or two things and having some global elements would be a real time saver.

description: when you require support, you always receive useful prompt response, solutions and workaround from them in a timely manner.
author: Kelcy P.
pros: AI assistance; features; integration to Sharepoint; ability to prefill fields; table fields; low cost; frequent updates and addition of new features; user friendly
cons: layout (unable to use customise fonts and limited selection of formatting for content cells); file size limitation when integrating to Sharepoint (it is not something that Cognito can do though); interface can be nicer; unable to copy & paste fields from one form to another; no bulk send from the platform; reminders cannot be sent to public users

description: I love cognito - it revolutionized the way I'm able to operate my organization and streamlined formerly arduous tasks.
author: Kieley S.
pros: ease of use - I feel like I learned a lot of different ways to use it in a very short time flexibility - I've been able to use the forms for a lot of different tasks that needed to be automated
cons: I would like to be able to copy information from one form to another form without having to copy the whole thing. I'd like the security options to apply to all possible categories instead of just a select few.

review_title: User-Friendly and Powerful Tool for Streamlining School Processes
description: Overall, my experience with Cognito Forms has been very positive. It’s a reliable and powerful tool that supports a wide range of needs, from simple forms to more complex systems. Once you get familiar with its capabilities, it becomes an incredibly efficient way to manage information and streamline processes. I enjoy building out forms with Cognito Forms!
author: Ashley J.
pros: Cognito Forms is pretty intuitive and easy to use, even for more complex workflows. The form builder is flexible, and I appreciate the ability to customize fields, calculations, and conditional logic. It’s been especially helpful for organizing school processes like sign-ups and data collection. I’ve also found tools like ChatGPT helpful for learning new features and improving how I use Cognito Forms more efficiently.
cons: Some of the more advanced features, like calculations or conditional logic, can take time to learn and aren’t always immediately intuitive. There are also some limitations in formatting and design customization compared to more design-focused platforms. At times, troubleshooting more complex forms requires a bit of trial and error.

description: ’ve had a great experience with Cognito Forms overall. It’s been a reliable and easy-to-use platform that helps me stay organized and manage my forms efficiently. I love how simple it is to create and customize forms to fit my needs, and it really helps streamline my workflow. Overall, it’s been a positive experience and a valuable tool for my business.
author: Candie W.
pros: What I liked most about Cognito Forms is how user-friendly and intuitive it is. It makes creating professional, customized forms quick and easy without needing technical experience.
cons: One downside of Cognito Forms is that the notification setup isn’t very straightforward. It can take some time to figure out how to properly set up email alerts, especially when you want to be notified every time someone submits a job application. The customization options are powerful, but they can feel a bit confusing at first, which makes simple things like receiving instant notifications harder than they should be.

review_title: Tax Preparation Intake Usage For Clients
description: Overall somewhat pleased with the product the difficulty returning to the same upload sheet for external clients is a major issue.
author: Bruce S.
pros: Simple to setup forms and make edits to information. Easy to coordinate work flow within the organization. All internal users quickly access and use the information provided.
cons: Very difficult for clients to return to the same upload document from an external source. External clients need to enter multiple forms if they have additional information.

author: Angela N.
pros: Cognito Forms has been such a lifesaver for me. I run multiple businesses and Cognito's templates made my, not to technology savvy self, create the most perfect registration forms so easy. I highly recomend them to anyone who asks.
cons: I wish that the formulas for the different sections were a little more easily accessible or easier to find.

review_title: Best option for building a form
author: Ashley C.
pros: Appreciate the variety of functions and the ability to connect seamlessly with multiple platforms.
cons: Occasionally, the website may experience some lag, and certain advanced options could benefit from further optimization or training to improve user experience

review_title: Great choice, simple to use, inexpensive.
description: I built simple forms and then moved all the way to developing an integrated form that led our customer facing data collection process.
author: Ben G.
pros: Cognito is one of the few form platforms out there that is intuitive to use, allows for the upload of forms and integrates into SharePoint.
cons: I have no down-sides of the platform. I’m a raving fan.

description: We use it daily to keep track of requests coming into the print shop.
author: Justin J.
pros: Cognito makes forms easy to set up, but offers more customization than you would expect.
cons: Sometimes the user interface doesn't allow certain actions (such as a multi-copy) and can take more clicks than it should to get somewhere.

description: It is the best tool to work as a team and with multiple clients in a form with a database.
author: Robert V.
pros: I can customize my forms however I want, it is a very persuasive advantage for my clients.
cons: Artificial intelligence does not generate forms correctly with any requirement that is requested, I suppose it is because it is in Beta version, I hope they improve that.

description: great overall experience using the free version
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: the features versus the cost is top notch
cons: you can use tons of the features for free

review_title: Cognito - Complex and useful form builder
description: It does the job it describes well - a highly customisable form builder with databases, APIs and front-end customisation as well.
author: Billy L.
pros: Cognito forms work as intended- the webpage loads fast, the forms fill in correctly, the results load, the API works. The forms also have a lot of customisability by using conditional logic (if/else statements) so you have a lot of options in creating your forms. This is extremely helpful for the sort of forms I need to create, and it works brilliantly if you have a technical/programming mind.
cons: The look isn't particularly appealing when you are filling in a form - I know that you can change the way the forms look on the front end but I think the default look could be upgraded to look somewhat more appealing The customer support isn't amazing through my limited interactions with them but luckily there aren't too many issues to fix
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I love CognitoForms and recommend it to all my clients
author: Cassie K.
pros: I love that it is so easy to use and that I can build fields with advanced formulas. It can do anything I need!
cons: I wish it would auto-save drafts of changes. Sometimes I've made a lot of updates to the form but havent saved them yet, but then something happens and I lose all my changes and have to start again.

description: Great! Would definitely recommend if you consistently need to collect information or feedback and want a professional experience for your customers.
author: Jessica R.
pros: I love that I can use Cognito Forms for reviews, for client feedback, for research, for applications, and interest forms. The forms are very easy to create, look great, and are easy to fill and submit.
cons: I still don't really understand how to get the most out of workflow, would love more access to how to videos ect.

author: Ville P.
pros: Cognito Forms offers beautiful and fast forms for data collection. They are easy to embed into your website content or use as they are. The great integration options and webhooks provide value even for more advanced users.
cons: More advanced users might find some aspects of it cumbersome to manage, but there's ways to work around them.

review_title: Excellent Functionality, Great Value for Money
description: Switched from another provider to Cognito and so glad we did. Functionality is great and cost of subscription is good value for money.
author: Gordon H.
pros: Very easy to use, excellent functionality. Great looking forms.
cons: Reporting on results is a bit basic. Results need to be manipulated in a spreadsheet.

description: It has made our admin systems move a lot smoother and simplified the forms we need to regularly have filled out properly ensuring all details are filled.
author: Tim B.
pros: I like how easy and effective it is to use.
cons: There is not much I do not like about it, potentially the UI could use some upgrades to make it more aesthetically pleasing

author: Shubhra S.
pros: The forms come handy for survey and data collection. It is easy to integrate with website
cons: The forms look and feel cannot be changed

review_title: Excellent product, nothing missing, solid customer support, tonnes of functionality.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The malleability of what you can do with these forms, is incredible. Basically think of what you want from a form, and there's a way you can do it.
cons: The KB could use some more realistic examples of use of code. That would make it easier to learn and improve my ability to use the product.

review_title: Very customizable, modern and extraordinary forms
author: Monika W.
pros: There is so much to set up! I use this to collect feedback on my cosmetic products - customers first choose the products they tried and accordingly the forms to the single products roll. This way I create all the questions and customers do not get overwhelmed by the huge amount of possibilities and I get a high rate of responses. Every field is programmable here. In my beginnings of setting up an e-shop - my first customers even placed an order through one such form - however, I did not use the option to pay with a card, but it is great they allow it.
cons: When I tried to embed the form on my e-shop and chose to use the formatting styles of the website, it did not look right and I had to use the font I set in the Cognitoforms. Also, it takes some time for one to learn how to use the software.

review_title: Cognito Forms is a great, inexpensive data system
description: I love that Cognito keeps on improving. I hope to see them continue making the user experience better. Thank you!
author: Glencora P.
pros: I have been using Cognito to collect data from two schools. The system is pretty intuitive, and once you have used it for awhile, a lot of things are clearer. For example, to name the columns or categories for when you are ready to export a file to excel, you can simply customize the field names, which helps a lot. The forms embed nicely on a website. The emails sent to customers is great. I love that I can work with others on these forms; that has been excellent. The Support for Cognito has been excellent; they are swift and comprehensive in their help. The forms really allow for you to do a lot.
cons: Some of the problems I have run into are the following: --placing an image in the form header has been tricky. I can't easily place it as I'd like. --I initially named the organization something, and I'd like to change it. I can't easily do that now because the form links are according to the original organization name. It certainly is possible, but it will take some time to fix. --Sometimes it's clear that the server is busy, and it does not work well on Safari (nothing really does, though) --I wish there was an easier way to have people make sure they paid for something before they filled out a form (which ultimately requires payment for submission). It's not clear how to do this.
